expectations.text: Applies the deletion test by name to the repository
expectations.evidence: No 'deletion test' as a named heuristic. Argues by listing wrapper costs and benefits.

expectations.text: Identifies the wrapper as shallow / pass-through / hypothetical seam
expectations.evidence: Calls it a 'thin wrapper' and says abstraction 'has not aged well' but does not use the named terms shallow / pass-through / hypothetical seam.
'Inline each call to the equivalent `_context.Warehouses.*` expression.'

expectations.text: Pushes back on 'swap EF Core later' as the one-adapter / hypothetical-seam anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Three concrete arguments: IQueryable leaks, EF Core not commodity, three years of zero swaps. Strong rebuttal but does not name the structural anti-pattern.

expectations.text: Identifies the real seam location: DbContext / EF Core provider level
expectations.evidence: Mentions in-memory SQLite / testcontainers as test setup but does not identify DbContext/EF Core provider as where the seam belongs structurally.

expectations.text: Uses at least 3 of the skill vocabulary terms
expectations.evidence: Uses 'wrapper', 'abstraction', 'indirection' but not seam/shallow/depth/leverage/locality/hypothetical as a vocabulary system.

expectations.text: Ends with 'Which of these would you like to explore?' or equivalent
expectations.evidence: Ends with 'Technically, there is no argument for keeping it.' — no workflow prompt.
